content:
# Disagreement Log — Numbers 27 (NUM_027)
**Arbiter:** SOPHIA | **In council with:** EMET, LOGOS, PNEUMA

---

### Verse 1
- **EMET:** "drew near -- and these [are] the names of his daughters: Mahlah, Noah, and Hoglah, and Milcah, and Tirzah" / "of the families of Manasseh"
- **LOGOS:** "drew near -- and these are the names of his daughters: Mahlah, Noah, Hoglah, Milcah, and Tirzah" / "of the clans of Manasseh"
- **PNEUMA:** "stepped forward" (restructured sentence, placing genealogy before the approach); "from the clans of Manasseh"; "These were the names"
- **Resolution:** SOPHIA adopts "drew near" (EMET/LOGOS), which is the standard rendering of Hebrew ותקרבנה, confirmed in both MT and LXX (προσῆλθον). "Clans" (LOGOS/PNEUMA) is preferred over "families" (EMET) for מִשְׁפְּחֹת, as "clan" better reflects the social unit between tribe and household. The parenthetical structure of EMET/LOGOS is preserved rather than PNEUMA's restructuring. "And" conjunctions between daughter names (EMET) are simplified to follow LOGOS/PNEUMA comma-list format.

### Verse 2
- **EMET:** "all the congregation, [at] the opening of the tent of meeting, saying:"
- **LOGOS:** "all the congregation, at the entrance of the tent of meeting, saying,"
- **PNEUMA:** "the entire community, at the entrance of the Tent of Meeting, and said:"
- **Resolution:** SOPHIA uses "all the congregation" (EMET/LOGOS) per project convention for עֵדָה. "Entrance" (LOGOS/PNEUMA) is preferred over "opening" (EMET) for פֶּתַח. "Tent of meeting" is lowercase (EMET/LOGOS). "Saying" is retained over PNEUMA's "and said" to preserve the Hebrew participial construction לֵאמֹר.

### Verse 3
- **EMET:** "he was not in the midst of the congregation, the ones who gathered themselves together against YHWH in the congregation of Korah, but in his [own] sin he died, and sons he did not have"
- **LOGOS:** "he was not among the congregation of those who gathered together against YHWH in the congregation of Korah, but he died in his own sin, and he had no sons"
- **PNEUMA:** "He was not among the faction that banded together against YHWH -- the faction of Korah -- but he died for his own sin, and he had no sons"
- **Resolution:** SOPHIA adopts "among the congregation of those who gathered themselves together" (blending EMET's reflexive "gathered themselves" with LOGOS's cleaner structure). "Congregation" is retained over PNEUMA's "faction" for עֵדָה. "In his own sin he died" preserves the Hebrew word order (בְּחֶטְאוֹ מֵת). "He had no sons" follows LOGOS/PNEUMA over EMET's more wooden "sons he did not have."

### Verse 4
- **EMET:** "be withdrawn from the midst of his family" / "Give to us a possession in the midst of the brothers of our father"
- **LOGOS:** "be withdrawn from the midst of his clan" / "Give to us a possession among the brothers of our father"
- **PNEUMA:** "disappear from his clan" / "Give us a land holding among our father's kinsmen"
- **Resolution:** SOPHIA uses "withdrawn" (EMET/LOGOS) for יִגָּרַע rather than PNEUMA's "disappear." "Clan" (LOGOS/PNEUMA) for מִשְׁפָּחָה over "family" (EMET). "Among the brothers of our father" (LOGOS) over EMET's "in the midst of" and PNEUMA's "kinsmen." "Possession" (EMET/LOGOS) over "land holding" (PNEUMA).

### Verse 5
- **EMET:** "their judgment"
- **LOGOS:** "their case"
- **PNEUMA:** "their case"
- **Resolution:** SOPHIA adopts "their case" (LOGOS/PNEUMA majority) for מִשְׁפָּטָן. "Case" better fits the context of a legal matter brought before YHWH for adjudication, since the judgment has not yet been rendered.

### Verse 7
- **EMET:** "Rightly the daughters of Zelophehad speak"
- **LOGOS:** "The daughters of Zelophehad speak rightly"
- **PNEUMA:** "The daughters of Zelophehad are right in what they say" / "You must certainly give them a hereditary land holding among their father's kinsmen, and transfer their father's inheritance to them"
- **Resolution:** SOPHIA adopts EMET's word order "Rightly the daughters of Zelophehad speak," preserving the emphatic fronting of כֵּן. "You shall surely give" (EMET/LOGOS) preserves the infinitive absolute נָתֹן תִּתֵּן. "Possession of an inheritance" (EMET/LOGOS) over "hereditary land holding" (PNEUMA). "You shall cause the inheritance of their father to pass over to them" (EMET/LOGOS) over PNEUMA's "transfer."

### Verse 8
- **EMET:** "If a man dies and he has no son"
- **LOGOS:** "If a man dies and has no son"
- **PNEUMA:** "When a man dies without leaving a son" / "speak to the Sons of Israel" (capitalized)
- **Resolution:** SOPHIA follows LOGOS's "If a man dies and has no son." PNEUMA's "When" changes the conditional אִם to temporal, and "without leaving" is interpretive. Lowercase "sons of Israel" per project convention (EMET/LOGOS).

### Verse 11
- **EMET:** "if there are no brothers to his father" / "his kinsman, the one nearest to him from his family"
- **LOGOS:** "if his father has no brothers" / "his kinsman who is nearest to him from his clan"
- **PNEUMA:** "if his father has no brothers" / "his nearest relative within his clan" / "a binding rule of law"
- **Resolution:** SOPHIA follows LOGOS: "if his father has no brothers" (LOGOS/PNEUMA majority). "Kinsman who is nearest to him from his clan" (LOGOS) balances literalness with clarity. "Statute of judgment" (EMET/LOGOS) over PNEUMA's "binding rule of law."

### Verse 12
- **EMET:** "see the land which I have given"
- **LOGOS:** "see the land that I have given"
- **PNEUMA:** "look out over the land that I have given" / "mountain of the Abarim range"
- **Resolution:** SOPHIA uses "see the land which I have given" (EMET). PNEUMA's "look out over" adds expansion not in Hebrew וּרְאֵה. "Mountain of Abarim" (EMET/LOGOS) over PNEUMA's "mountain of the Abarim range."

### Verse 13
- **EMET:** "And you shall see it, and you shall be gathered to your people, also you, as Aaron your brother was gathered"
- **LOGOS:** "And when you have seen it, you also shall be gathered to your people, as Aaron your brother was gathered"
- **PNEUMA:** "And when you have seen it, you too will be gathered to your people, just as your brother Aaron was gathered"
- **Resolution:** SOPHIA adopts LOGOS's rendering. "When you have seen it" (LOGOS/PNEUMA) captures the Hebrew sequence better. "You also" (LOGOS) preserves גַּם אַתָּה. "As Aaron your brother" (EMET/LOGOS) preserves Hebrew word order over PNEUMA's "your brother Aaron."

### Verse 14
- **EMET:** "because you rebelled against my word" / "These [are] the waters of Meribah of Kadesh, [in] the wilderness of Zin"
- **LOGOS:** "because you rebelled against my word" / "These are the waters of Meribah of Kadesh, in the wilderness of Zin"
- **PNEUMA:** "because you both rebelled against my command" / "you failed to uphold my holiness" / "(These are the waters of Meribah-kadesh, in the wilderness of Zin.)"
- **Resolution:** SOPHIA follows EMET/LOGOS: "my word" for פִּי, and "to sanctify me" for לְהַקְדִּישֵׁנִי. PNEUMA adds "both" (not explicit in the Hebrew), paraphrases "to sanctify me" as "to uphold my holiness," and hyphenates "Meribah-kadesh." "Meribah of Kadesh" (EMET/LOGOS) better reflects the construct chain מְרִיבַת קָדֵשׁ.

### Verse 16
- **EMET:** "the God of the spirits of all flesh"
- **LOGOS:** "the God of the spirits of all flesh"
- **PNEUMA:** "the God of the spirits of all living beings" / "appoint someone over the community"
- **Resolution:** SOPHIA retains "all flesh" (EMET/LOGOS) for כָל־בָּשָׂר, the standard biblical idiom. "A man" (EMET/LOGOS) over PNEUMA's "someone" for אִישׁ. "Congregation" over "community" per convention.

### Verse 17
- **EMET:** "and the congregation of YHWH shall not be as sheep which have no shepherd"
- **LOGOS:** "so that the congregation of YHWH may not be as sheep that have no shepherd"
- **PNEUMA:** "so that the community of YHWH will not be like sheep without a shepherd"
- **Resolution:** SOPHIA adopts "so that" (LOGOS/PNEUMA) as a purpose clause. "Shall not be" retains the stronger modal. "Sheep which have no shepherd" (EMET) preserves the relative clause אֲשֶׁר אֵין־לָהֶם רֹעֶה over PNEUMA's simplified "sheep without a shepherd."

### Verse 18
- **EMET:** "Take to yourself Joshua son of Nun, a man in whom [is] spirit, and you shall lay your hand upon him"
- **LOGOS:** "Take for yourself Joshua son of Nun, a man in whom is the spirit, and lay your hand upon him"
- **PNEUMA:** "Take Joshua son of Nun, a man in whom the spirit dwells, and lay your hand upon him"
- **Resolution:** "Take for yourself" (LOGOS) for לְךָ. "A man in whom is spirit" omits "the" before "spirit" (following EMET), since Hebrew רוּחַ is anarthrous and the ambiguity is theologically significant. PNEUMA's "dwells" adds interpretation. "And lay" (LOGOS/PNEUMA imperative) over EMET's "and you shall lay."

### Verse 19
- **EMET:** "And you shall stand him" / "you shall commission him before their eyes"
- **LOGOS:** "And set him" / "commission him before their eyes"
- **PNEUMA:** "And stand him" / "commission him in their sight"
- **Resolution:** SOPHIA adopts "set him" (LOGOS) for the hiphil of עמד. "Before their eyes" (EMET/LOGOS) over "in their sight" (PNEUMA) for לְעֵינֵיהֶם.

### Verse 20
- **EMET:** "put from your splendor upon him" / "may hear [him]"
- **LOGOS:** "put some of your splendor upon him" / "may obey"
- **PNEUMA:** "invest him with some of your authority" / "will obey him"
- **Resolution:** "Some of your splendor" (LOGOS) renders the partitive מִן naturally while preserving "splendor" for הוֹד (EMET/LOGOS) over PNEUMA's "authority." "May obey" (LOGOS) for יִשְׁמְעוּ over EMET's "hear," as context requires obedience. PNEUMA's "invest him with" restructures too freely.

### Verse 21
- **EMET:** "And before Eleazar the priest he shall stand, and he shall inquire for him"
- **LOGOS:** "And before Eleazar the priest he shall stand, and he shall inquire for him"
- **PNEUMA:** "And he shall stand before Eleazar the priest, who will seek guidance for him"
- **Resolution:** EMET/LOGOS agree exactly, preserving the fronted word order. PNEUMA restructures to standard English and paraphrases "inquire" as "seek guidance." SOPHIA follows EMET/LOGOS.

### Verse 22
- **EMET:** "And he took Joshua and stood him before Eleazar"
- **LOGOS:** "And he took Joshua and set him before Eleazar"
- **PNEUMA:** "He took Joshua and stood him before Eleazar" (drops initial "And")
- **Resolution:** SOPHIA uses "set him" (LOGOS) for consistency with verse 19. Initial "And" retained (EMET/LOGOS) per convention.

### Verse 23
- **EMET:** "as YHWH spoke by the hand of Moses"
- **LOGOS:** "as YHWH spoke by the hand of Moses"
- **PNEUMA:** "just as YHWH had spoken through Moses"
- **Resolution:** SOPHIA follows EMET/LOGOS, preserving the Hebrew idiom בְּיַד מֹשֶׁה literally ("by the hand of Moses"). PNEUMA's "through Moses" paraphrases the idiom, and "had spoken" adds a pluperfect tense not required by the Hebrew.

---
*Produced by SOPHIA in council with EMET, LOGOS, and PNEUMA*
